items.title: Court shutdown to end after Department of Justice resolves security dispute

children.content: Regional West Australian courts closed amid a stoush over security services are set to reopen about a month earlier than originally planned.

Exterior shot of the Fitzroy Crossing courthouse in bright sunshine.

items.title: Hastie talks tough on One Nation while Taylor hesitates

image.altText: Andrew Hastie stands in a crowd of people wearing a suit and tie with a sticker showing his name.

children.content: As Angus Taylor grapples with the Coalition's dilemma of how to keep the centre while diverting voters away from Pauline Hanson's party, Andrew Hastie lays out his vision for fighting One Nation.

items.title: Woman attacked by pack of dogs says owners did nothing to help

children.content: A woman who says she was attacked by a pack of dogs that were being walked off-leash in Western Australia's far north is calling for more responsible pet ownership.

thumbnail.altText: Street sign with long road with car approaching bend in background

items.title: Family still reeling after death of baby in regional WA

children.content: The parents of Willow Horne are still looking for answers after the eight-month-old died in a regional WA hospital.

image.altText: A baby with hands on top of balancing ball

items.title: Report finds 'extensive' coal and gas presence in schools and children's sport

children.content: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

image.altText: Children look at a laptop. The word Glencore can be seen on the lid. A person in an orange high-vis shirt is nearby.

items.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row

children.content: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant 51-point victory over Gold Coast at Perth Stadium — a result that puts them two games clear at the top of the AFL ladder.

gallery.altText: Luke Jackson celebrates an AFL goal with a Dockers teammate.

thumbnail.altText: Luke Jackson celebrates an AFL goal with a Dockers teammate.

items.title: Disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service resumes after years of delays

children.content: The disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service is back on track following years of breakdowns and delays, but locals remain frustrated over the rollout.

items.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths in record European heatwave

children.content: More than 150 million Europeans swelter in "extreme heat", which scientists say has been the worst the continent has ever recorded.

image.altText: Men, women and children bathing in fountain water in front of the Eiffel Tower.

items.title: Ben Stokes's retirement a perfect example of his main character energy

children.content: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

items.title: Expanded World Cup called 'vulgar and ordinary' by Ghana coach. Is that fair?

children.content: Ghana's coach Carlos Queiroz has lashed out at FIFA's expanded 48-team World Cup, despite his team progressing. Is his criticism justified as the tournament moves into the knockout stage?

articles.title: Quick hits: Canada's emotional match-winner and scenes in rural Kansas

children.content: Canada's heroes tread new ground, South Korea's extreme reaction to going out and scenes in Algeria's home away from home. Here's the five quick hits from the World Cup.

articles.title: Canada 'heroes' score stunning injury time winner to down South Africa

children.content: World Cup co-host Canada beats South Africa to progress into the last 16 thanks to a last-gasp strike from Stephen Eustaquio in Los Angeles.

items.title: Thai teenager's father 'deeply saddened' as Australian charged with murder

children.content: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

items.title: Williams takes aim at anti-doping measures ahead of Wimbledon return

children.content: As she prepares for her All England Club comeback, Serena Williams claims the tennis anti-doping protocol is "unprofessional" and "unreasonable".

items.title: Key observatory shut for months prior to bird flu detection

children.content: Volunteers and wildlife advocates push to reopen the Eyre Bird Observatory, arguing the station will be critical in monitoring the potential impact of avian flu.

items.title: NASA to bump telescope back into space to avoid it falling to Earth

children.content: US space agency NASA is racing to save an aging telescope, used to observe cosmic explosions, from falling back to Earth with a daring rescue mission.

items.title: How Ben overcame his dilemma about disclosing his autism in job interviews

children.content: The cybersecurity sector is projected to need tens of thousands of roles filled by 2030. Now, a university-based program is helping provide those workers by tapping into the abilities of autistic people.

items.title: 'Unlucky being born as Iranian': Diaspora's social fabric under strain

children.content: Iranian Australians are grappling with mistrust, harassment and isolation within a community deeply traumatised by an oppressive regime.

items.title: Two things this week gave clues about the global future

items.title: Real estate has been an easy target for criminals laundering money, until now

children.content: Anti-money laundering laws mean that for the first time, real estate agents, lawyers, accountants and others must report suspicious transactions.

items.title: Major capital cities record worst housing auction clearance rates in years

children.content: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

image.altText: An A-frame sign that reads "auction today", directing toward a house.

items.title: WA shearing teams look for new ways to make ends meet

children.content: Almost 100 shearing shed workers across regional WA sign up to a new training program aimed at helping contractors and their teams find work beyond the shearing industry.
